steps.title: Find the width of the rectangular base
steps.content:
The base has area $56t$ cm² and length $\frac{14}{3}$ cm. Use the formula base area = length × width:

$$56t = \frac{14}{3} \times w$$

Solve for $w$:
$$w = 56t \div \frac{14}{3} = 56t \times \frac{3}{14} = \frac{168t}{14} = 12t \text{ cm}$$

steps.title: Calculate surface area with all three dimensions
steps.content:
Now we have all dimensions: $l = \frac{14}{3}$ cm, $w = 12t$ cm, $h = 3$ cm. The surface area formula is $\text{SA} = 2(lw + lh + wh)$. Calculate each face area:
  • $lw = \frac{14}{3} \times 12t = 56t$
  • $lh = \frac{14}{3} \times 3 = 14$
  • $wh = 12t \times 3 = 36t$
Add them up and double:
$$\text{SA} = 2(56t + 14 + 36t) = 2(92t + 14) = 184t + 28$$

That matches choice B — B is correct.
